title: plantago alpina subsp. incana
intro: Plantago alpina subsp. incana, also known as the woolly plantain, is a subspecies of the perennial plantain species. It is a small, herbaceous plant with woolly leaves and stems, commonly found in alpine regions. Plantago alpina subsp. incana has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which make it useful for treating various health issues, including urinary tract infections, wounds, and respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and it is also used in some herbal preparations for its expectorant properties to help alleviate coughs and congestion.
